question_text: A parachutist jumps from a plane. At the moment of jump, weight = 900 N and air resistance = 100 N. After 40 seconds, weight = 900 N and air resistance = 900 N. Draw a free body diagram for each moment and explain which moment represents balanced forces and which represents unbalanced forces.
correct_answer: At jump: weight (900 N down) and air resistance (100 N up) are unbalanced, resultant = 800 N down. After 40 seconds: weight (900 N down) and air resistance (900 N up) are balanced, resultant = 0 N. Unbalanced forces cause acceleration downward; balanced forces mean the parachutist has reached terminal velocity and moves at constant speed.
explanation: Free body diagrams show all forces acting on an object. When forces are unbalanced, there is acceleration. When they are balanced, the object moves at constant velocity or stays still.
